desc:
The Chieftain's quarter is more elaborately decorated and spacious compared to
the rest of the Dunlending camp. A large bed lined with rare white furs and soft
leather sits at the far end of the room. Hung on the walls of the hut are
personal trophies of the chieftain. The most noticeable trophy is a necklace
made of brownish mountain-lion teeth in which each tooth represents proof of the
renowned hunting abilities the chieftain possesses.
name: Chieftain's Quarters

desc:
This dark smelly room is crowded with weapons and tools of all shapes and sizes.
Stacks of sharp flint tipped spears rest along the western wall. Slings made of
soft leather hang in great bundles on nails hammered into the walls. Bows of
different thicknesses lie against heaps of sharpened arrows with small stone
tips. The flint-worker has been hard at work keeping this room well stocked with
weapons.
name: Storage Hut

desc:
Water basins, broken clay jars and empty bowls are scattered on the dirty floor,
along with a stone anvil that was once used to crush wheat. This room, which
used to be the kitchen of the palace, was designed to feed dozens of people at
the same time. However, the kitchen has been pillaged and now looks as if a
storm passed through it. The western wall, above the fireplace, is darkened by
the huge fire which burned all the wooden furniture that once decorated this
room.
name: Ruined Kitchen

desc:
The main element of the room is this huge stone table, nearly 100 feet long,
broken in its middle by a strong blow. In this place the former inhabitants of
these halls had great banquets, and daily meals too. Four water bowls are at the
four corners of the piece, dry now that the tubes have been filled up by the
mud.
name: Hall of Celebration

desc:
This immense Hall extends so far in every direction that it seems endless, for
no walls are visible from here. The domed ceiling is supported by an seemingly
infinite range of white pillars. Bas-reliefs were sculpted on them, but most
have been scrupulously destroyed by heavy hammer blows. On those carvings that
remain a fleet of four large ships can be recognized, each ship having a white
tree figured on every sail.
name: Hall of Celebration

desc:
Lines after lines, dozens of carved pillars create an atmosphere of geometric
beauty. Several of these columns are indeed sculptures representing the wise
personalities who once walked in this hostel. Among those that can be recognized
are Celebrimbor, builder of the palace, Galadriel and Celeborn who lived in
Eregion for several centuries, and Aldarion with his captains, who first crossed
the sea to visit the Noldor. While many of the statues have been hammered, they
still stand. Now the elves are gone, cheated and hunted by Annatar, and only the
lost souls of his fallen soldiers haunt these halls.
name: Hall of Celebration

desc:
In the middle of this colossal room stands an enormous pillar, which seems to
enlarge the already large hall. The pillar is circled by a ring of stone tables,
most of which have been destroyed. This hall, which must be more than 30 feet
high, and no less than 3 furlongs long, has an extension east to a similar but
somewhat smaller hall. The room continues north through through a narrow but
extremely long tunnel.
name: Hall of Celebration

desc:
You stand in what is still a beautifully painted hall, with arched pillars to
support the sculpted ceiling and marble benches around them. A high chair of
pink marble dominates the room, before a wall of white stone streaked with
silver. The ruin that fell on the place centuries ago was not sufficient to
destroy the throne, but all the other stone furniture lay half broken on the
floor.
name: Throne Room

desc:
This room, while big is empty, except for some garbage which litter the floor.
It is a very long and wide hall, several dozens feet in each direction. The
floor is marked by perpendicular lines, dug a few centimeters into the ground.
These lines are all that remains of wooden panels which once split this hall in
separate apartments. Now the inhabitants are gone, and the place is haunted by
the souls of the evil humans who died here under Sauron's leadership.
name: Living Quarters

desc:
You stand in what used to be a workshop of the palace. Here, tools were mended
and weapons were forged, and maybe even rings created. This room, now blackened
from the fire which destroyed everything, once had a burning forge which emitted
smoke through the chimney, while fresh air flowed through the small conduits.
Both the conduits and the chimney are now filled with earth preventing exits
through either one.
name: Burned Workshop

desc:
The long tunnel comes to a sudden end here, as the ceiling has collapsed under
the weight of wet earth. Tiny bits of dirt fall on your head from spots in the
unsupported ceiling, but roots seem to be holding together most of the ceiling
preventing a total collapse. The passage east is blocked by a mass of earth and
loose rocks.
name: Damp Cavern

desc:
This tunnel, a dozen feet wide and high enough for a rider and his horse, is
obviously artificial in construction, as the walls are straight and the floor
level. However, the tunnel is partially filled with earth and moss is growing on
the side indicating the tunnel is very old and probably abandoned. The tunnel
extends to the north and west.
name: Damp Passageway

desc:
Threadbare stairs connect the surface with a deep passage. These stairs are
enshrouded by earth and vegetation, and are recognizable as stairs only because
recent rains have washed away some of the debris. They appear to be made of
white marble, polished by many years of use.
name: Worn Stairs

desc:
A long tunnel extends before you, dug in the calcarian rocks under the hills of
Eregion. The floor had been expensively decorated by a mosaic, which displays
the opulence of the former landlords. But in many places it has been mangled by
evil hands, and muddy water flowing from the demolished ceiling partially covers
it.
name: Wet Corridor

desc:
While to the south an extremely long corridor extends, you stand now in the
crushed portal leading in a white Hall. The gate is more than five meters high,
and was closed by two huge copper doors. They have been smashed by a outstanding
force, and only one is still sadly suspended to the gate.
name: Entrance of the Friendship Hall

desc:
Before you is a very wide Hall, dug in the hard rocks under Eregion. The walls
are decorated with panels of white marble, and portentous sculptures have been
carved in tablets of obsidian. On either side, carved pillars extend along the
length of the room. Most of the inscriptions can still be read on tablets.
name: Friendship Hall

desc:
You are standing in the white Friendship Hall, where Tar-Aldarion met Galadriel
and Celeborn five thousands years ago. Magnificent sculptures have been carved
in tablets of obsidian and carved pillars extend along the central alley. The
hall has been devastated by Sauron's armies, and several broken marble tables
lay here and there, scattered around the floor. The place is empty, as all the
valuables items have been looted, and the wooden or silken furniture decayed.
name: Friendship Hall

desc:
This southern end of the large hall looks much the same as the rest of it, all
inlaid with the same black rock. An eerie feeling hangs in the whole throne
room, seemingly thick enough to cut if you chose to. A few statues line the
walls here, all of them of mighty warriors wielding strange weapons.
name: Obsidian Room

desc:
While mostly flat, small dips and rises can be seen in the surrounding
landscape. A healthy layer of grass covers everything, as if reluctant to let
other types of vegetation grow in the fertile soil. The bright blooms of flowers
make patches of colour against the otherwise green ground. The air is fresh
here, free from the confusing mixture of smells that herald civilization.
name: Green Downs

desc:
Long slopes and valleys, none of which are more than the height of a man,
stretch on for miles in every direction. The ground is covered with a thick
layer of grass, which is soft and springy to tread on. No flowers or shrubs grow
here, as there is little protection from the elements. Small holes dot the area,
perhaps the homes of foxes or woodchucks.
name: Wide Downs

desc:
The ground is mostly flat here, making it possible to see for miles in every
direction. Wild grasses are plentiful, covering every inch of landscape with a
thick carpet of green. Bushes grow sparsely, with little to shield them from the
wind in this wide open area.
name: Green Plains

desc:
This is an entrance to a narrow crooked path that provides passage between the
steep inclines of the Misty Mountains. Scars can be seen along the walls of the
passage, where the rock was painfully chiselled away. The pass appears to be
ageless, cut from solid granite and shielded from environmental weathering by
the towering mountains. However, crusty remains of tools and shifts in the
mountain itself indicate the passage is hundreds if not thousands of years old.
name: Mouth of a Mountain Pass

desc:
Embedded deep into the granite mountain, the pass continues both west and south.
A sheer cliff lies to the east while to the north the mountain wall blocks the
scenery and access to the valley floor. While much effort and care went into
construction of the passage, how it came to be is indeterminable.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
This pass, on the fringes of the Misty Mountains, is narrow and crooked. To the
east, both north and south, the mountains continue, blocking much of your
vision, while to the southwest the region where the mountains turn to plains is
visible. The pass itself is embedded deep into the rock for each step was
painfully chiselled into the rough terrain. While the exact age of the pass is
unknown, it appears to predate the flight of people, now known as Dunlendings,
who chose to settle the area now known as Bree.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
Cut deep into the granite rock, the narrow passage winds its way through the
Misty Mountains. All around the mountains loom above blocking much of your
vision of the surrounding terrain. To the east the pass approaches a large
crater while to the west the pass continues as it winds through the mountains
and later to the base of an ancient forest.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
Some thirty feet deep, the crater is largely formed of granite sprinkled with a
few glistening pieces of silicate. While the exact origin of the crater can not
be determined, it was clearly created by an internal force pushing out towards
the surface. Yet the granite rock contains little evidence of being exposed to
extreme heat, indicating that the crater was not caused by volcanic activity.
The bottom of the crater contains a large lake which appears to absorb all light
giving it a silky black appearance.
name: Craggy Crater

desc:
The lake is very dark, almost like a black pearl. While the surface is very
still, a slight undertow can be felt. A small ripple here and there gives an
impression of something swimming across these dark waters.
name: A Dark Lake

desc:
You have entered a large underwater cavern. A strong current flows over the
muddy bottom towards the east causing the water to turn a murky brown which
makes it difficult to see your surroundings.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
This chamber is narrower towards the east but begins to widen towards the north
and west. The walls are very smooth, almost unnaturally smooth. The chamber is
completely empty and the floor is hard rock. There is a very strong current
towards the east.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
This is a very large submerged cavern with a low ragged roof. While there is an
obvious exit towards the east, a faint westerly current can be felt. There are
many small rocks scattered across the uneven floor of the cavern.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
You have reached what appears to be the eastern end of the underwater cavern, as
there is no obvious way to continue onwards. There is a slight current dragging
you towards the east. Luckily, the ceiling has caved in, allowing you to reach
the surface above you.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
You have reached the surface of a smallish lake. Looking around, it is obvious
that this chamber was not created by natural forces. Extensive power must have
been expanded in the chamber's creation for the walls are too well rounded and
the chamber too secluded to have been formed by nature. Yet, clearly, no
humanoid life could have wielded the power needed to carve this chamber out of
the solid granite rock which forms the Misty Mountains. To the west a small
corridor lies beyond the lake's boundary.
name: Underground Lake

desc:
This simple corridor, some four feet long and eight feet high, is carved out of
solid granite. The walls of the corridor are featureless, smooth as silk and
cold to the touch. The only distinctive feature of this corridor is the solid
granite door at the western end.
name: Short Corridor

desc:
The cavern comes to a quick end forming a small snug chamber. The water is dead
still and the bottom is littered with rocks. On the sides, a very faint
engraving can be seen.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
You find yourself in a vast underground cavern at the top of an immense chasm;
an eerie glow makes the ceiling of the cavern visible. The chasm is no less than
two hundred yards deep. Looking across the abyss, you can see a trail embedded
in solid stone which seems to slowly wind down towards the bottom. It is not
obvious if the chasm was formed within the mountain or if the mountain was
formed over the chasm. In either case, the chasm appears as old as the mountain
itself.
name: Top of the Chasm

desc:
You find yourself on a small pathway that seems to lead into the bowels of the
Misty Mountains. While the path was originally chiselled into the granite floor,
over time, a millennium or more, it has slowly sunken forming a natural contour
in the rock.
name: Pathway

desc:
The path, set deep into the granite rock which forms the Misty Mountains, curves
to the south, but a deep and narrow chasm makes it unwise to continue in that
direction. The northern wall of the cavern rises some twenty yards before
forming a ceiling above you. To the east, beyond a granite door, there is a dark
lake and to the west the entrance of a small natural cavern can be seen.
Scattered about to the side of the path there are several rocks of various
sizes.
name: Pathway

desc:
The entrance into this cavern is from the east. Here, the rocky ceiling is very
low and damp, inhibiting your movements. While the air is very stale, some algae
have managed to grow on the far wall. The cavern continues to the south.
name: Entrance to a Small Cavern

desc:
The chamber here is very low and damp. Water drips from the ceiling and runs
across your face and into your eyes, making it difficult to see. A strong foetid
smell of decaying flesh and decomposing matter comes from the west riding an
outward air current.
name: Small Cavern

desc:
At the back of the small chamber, the cavern opens up somewhat allowing a bit
more room for movement. The only exit is the one you came through. On the floor
of the cavern there are many bones, most of which have either petrified or
started to turn to dust, and the dry husk of a strange wormlike creature.
name: Back Chamber

desc:
The trail that leads towards the bottom of the chasm has been moulded into solid
granite. A dim light seems to luminesce from below, making much of the winding
trail faintly visible, though sections of the trail remain hidden in the
shadows. There is an eerie feeling as shadows criss-cross the path and
blood-chilling howls echo across the walls. The bottom of the chasm is visible
to the east, and seems to be the source of the light. The trail continues to the
south. To the north a breach in the path is visible.
name: Start of the Chasm Trail

desc:
The trail bends sharply from north to west, avoiding the chasm to the east and
south. Whereas to the south, where the trail turns from west to north, a barrier
of solid granite blocks the view of the chasm, to the east the bottom of the
chasm can be seen as well as the trail winding unto itself. Across the chasm and
beyond the winding trail, the opening of a large cavern is barely discernible in
the low light. To the west, beyond an overhang, a much smaller opening can be
seen.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
This portion of the trail is dug into the western wall of the mountain as it
winds around the chasm. From here, the chasm and much of the path are hidden by
the overhang, but to the south an entrance to a cavern can be seen in the
shadows.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
Here, the path runs along the western edge of the chasm, forming a passage deep
inside the Misty Mountains. Even in darkness, shadows appear to whip across the
floor of the trail. To the west, a narrow opening in the granite wall appears to
beckon for exploration.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
The cavern is damp and gloomy, mostly hidden from the light that emanates from
the chasm to the east. Crouched in the narrow opening of the cavern one can
either return to the chasm trail which lies east or head into the cavern by
going north, south or west.
name: Cavern Opening

desc:
This section of the cavern is much wider than the section to the east where the
entrance lies. The walls are jagged, indicating that their formation was due to
a natural process, possibly an earthquake. If this cavern was indeed created by
an earthquake, then it is not clear why it did not extend to the surface. In
stark contrast to the walls, the roof and floor of the cavern are smooth and
level. The cavern extends to the west where it narrows.
name: Cavern

desc:
Deep inside the cavern, you are fully isolated from the outside environment. The
chamber is somewhat warm, sheltered from the cold which surrounds the chasm
trail. Although natural light has never reached this cavern, a soft glow comes
from the western wall.
name: Cavern

desc:
The air here is a bit cooler than to the north and as the water trickles in from
the north it begins to freeze, making the floor very slippery. A cool breeze
wafts in from the west. In the eastern corner one can see a small spider-web.
name: Cavern

desc:
A cold breeze blows in from the west making anyone who enters this cavern shiver
uncontrollably. This section of the cavern is very narrow and slick. The walls
are jagged, and could easily scrape off a few layers of skin should someone rub
against them. It appears that an earthquake sometime in the distant past had
sealed the passage to the west and opened a passage to the lower chambers.
name: Cavern

desc:
You find yourself in a tight pit, its walls coated with ice from the water that
once trickled down. While it is possible to climb up, it will take a great deal
of effort. The floor is littered with rubble and bodies of those who were unable
to escape this pit.
name: Tight Cavern Pit

desc:
Jumping over the rocks, climbing among the boulders, rolling on the screenings,
one should be able to slowly make the way through the cavern. Now and then the
mountain lets out a gasp and a large stone falls from the ceiling.
name: A Dislocated Cavern

desc:
This had once been a huge cavern with a flat ceiling and a floor polished by
running water. Stalactites and stone draperies once hung here, forming
impressive white pillars where they met the largest stalagmites. While some of
the stalagmites are still discernible, most have been destroyed, crushed by
falling granite blocks. The destruction must have occurred many years ago, as
new stalagmites have already begun to form.
name: A Huge Dislocated Cavern

desc:
Before this cavern was ripped apart by a tremor, this was once the junction of
an east-west passage with a north-south passage. Falling boulders and crumbling
granite now block passage to the north and west. However, one can still move
freely to the south and east.
name: A Dislocated Cavern

desc:
The icy breeze and a chilling murmur paralyze your body, making each movement
more difficult. Looking down, you can see the floor of the cavern which is
covered by mounds of ice. The ice diffuses what little light is available,
creating shadows across the far wall of the cavern. From the corner of your eye,
you see silhouette shapes dancing in the shadows where the chasm trail meets the
cavern floor.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
Several humanoid silhouettes appear to be standing here, partially hidden in the
shadows. Some are wielding swords and maces, but none are moving. A closer
observation reveals that they are packed in a crust of ice. Frozen trolls, orcs,
and one emaciated human have all been submerged by an even colder wave of frost
and turned into ice. A bright light, diffused and hidden, can be seen glowing
beneath the ice.
name: Silhouettes on the Trail

desc:
This chamber is littered with soft glowing moss. Perhaps it is the odd
combination of temperature and humidity which allows this moss to grow in
darkness or maybe the creator of this area grew the moss with the aid of magic.
There is probably no sure way to know how the moss evolved. As you linger here
the moss seems to start growing at an accelerated rate while slowly creeping
around your feet and up your legs as it begins to gnaw at your clothing and
skin.
name: Mossy Chamber

desc:
This side of the mountain is a sheer cliff providing no handholds or platform to
stand. In all directions but to the north where the border of an ancient forest
can be found, the rough mountains surround you. To the west and south an ancient
pass that provides passage through the mountain is visible. Unfortunately, as
you begin to fall down the north side you realise that you will not be able to
reach the pass in time.
name: A Sheer Cliff

desc:
Having just fallen off the top of the cliff, you begin your rather quick decent
towards the bottom of the chasm. As you fall you can see the long trail as it
circles around the chasm. Ouch, this fall is really going to hurt!
name: Falling into the Chasm

desc:
Having arrived at a dead end, the only way back is to the south. This cavern is
very damp and the air here is a bit warmer than to the south. The source of the
warmth is not obvious and the warmth itself may very well be the lack of
coldness which emanates from the chasm. In the far corner of the cavern there
appears to be a small nest.
name: Cavern

desc:
This room is composed of solid ice. The far and near walls of the chamber are
lined with blocks of ice no less than two yards deep. While it is not obvious
what might have inhabited this chamber, if anything, it certainly is much too
cold for humanoid life. The only exit from this chamber is back towards the
east.
name: Icy Chamber

desc:
From the top southwest corner of the trail, the entrances to the caverns on the
northern and eastern walls are discernible in the dim light. Looking carefully,
shadows seem to be flickering in and out, even though no source of them can be
seen.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
This segment of the chasm trail runs east-west. The northern wall is silky
smooth, indicating that its formation was unnatural in origin. The wall is
extremely cold to the touch, causing an odd sensation against the warm air from
above.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
Hugging the souther wall of the chasm, the trail slopes sharply downwards
towards the east. An intermittent rustling sound, from the granite floor below,
can be faintly heard as it is distorted by an icy wind flowing from the east.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
This is the southeast corner of the trail. To the northeast, looking over the
chasm, a large opening to another of the caverns along the trail is visible. The
trail continues to slope downwards towards the north, encouraging you to proceed
in that direction.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
This portion of the path, along the eastern wall, is covered with ice making
movement very difficult. Great care must be taken if you are to avoid slipping
and falling into the chasm below. Careful examination of the ice shows scrape
marks where those before you have slipped and fallen. There is a small hole in
the middle of the path where the granite floor, the ceiling of a cavern below,
has given way.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
The pit is shallow and narrow, some eleven feet deep and four feet wide. While
the ground contains a few partial skeletons of smaller animals, the sides of the
pit are rough and provide many handholds, making it possible for an average
climber to escape via the western wall.
name: A Pit

desc:
The odour of urine and blood permeates the air of the dark cavern. The floor is
covered with broken bones and half-decayed corpses. Some five yards up, through
a hole in the ceiling, the chasm trail can be seen. However, there appears to be
no way to reach the hole, and therefore the chasm trail, from here.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
From this portion of the cavern a foetid stench emanates from deposits of of
urea and excrement. Pulverized earth has been dropped on the granite floor, now
saturated with urine. Dark lines are visible on the floor where the previous
polluted earth has been brushed south, through a grid, behind which must be a
makeshift sewer of some sort.
name: Smelly Corner

desc:
Decapitated corpses and partially eaten bones litter the granite floor, while
dry blood and some which is not so dry, is splattered across the walls.
Undoubtedly, many a feasts have been held here; probably one was fairly recent
as is evident from the fresh blood stains. Most of the corpses are those of
orcs, but a few aging human and elf bones are identifiable.
name: Bloody Corner

desc:
You are at a junction in the dark cavern. It is quite easy to get disoriented
here as the darkness masks all sense of direction. To make matters worse, a
strange mist fills the room making you feel dizzy and disoriented.
name: Dark Cavern Junction

desc:
Covered with moisture, the walls of this chamber are colder and damper than the
adjoining chambers. Though slightly above freezing, the chamber emits an
unnatural coldness not felt on the icy path of the chasm trail. The ceiling of
the chamber is littered with icy stalactites that slowly drip onto the floor
creating a low fog across the chamber.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
Against the far wall, the skulls of orcs are piled in a very regular order. To
the side of this pile, near the granite wall, the remains of several broken orc
skeletons can be seen with a hand there, a toe here all scattered in irregular
fashion.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
Pieces of clothing, dry bone and clean sand are dispersed across the floor,
creating a lair where a beasts might take a nap between feasts. This part of the
cavern is exceptionally clean, and a small pond has formed where some of the ice
has melted at the foot of the eastern wall.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
The remains of excrement, brushed through the grid to the north, slowly decays.
Decaying bones and moist earth are piled in every corner, leaching a smell that
even orcs would find offensive. A small hole, through which the liquid waste
pours, is partially filled with dirty mud.
name: Sewage

desc:
At the foot of the granite wall a dark maw opens, partially lined with sharp
rocks giving it the appearance of giant teeth. The odour of decaying flesh
slowly seeps out from the opening in large puffs, almost as if the cavern were a
living entity breathing through its mouth. The cold ambient air makes it
difficult for you to move.
name: Junction in the Chasm Trail

desc:
The trail, cut a few yards into the southern wall, descends southward where the
low rocky ceiling masks a dark cavern. Small stalactites have formed on the
ceiling long ago when water still flowed, but now the stalactites are grey and
have ceased to grow. At the base of the wall, a small crack lined with cracked
mud and ice is all that remains of an ancient spring.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
Having just fallen into the chasm midway down the trail, you begin your rather
quick decent towards the bottom of the chasm. As you fall you can see the long
trail as it circles around the chasm. Ouch, this fall is going to hurt!
name: Falling into the Chasm

desc:
Looping around in the darkness, the trail seems to go on forever. At its deepest
point, a cloud of darkness appears to be darting back and forth in the chasm.
All that passes under the darkness vanishes from your view to never reappear.
Who knows what your fate will be if the shadow passes over you.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
A platform has been etched into the rough granite and from the platform a few
steps lead upwards to the north. From this location, one can see and contrast
the massive walls of the chasm with the tiny trail. It must have taken enormous
effort, far greater than one such as yourself could provide in many lifetimes,
to construct this cavern.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
This is the northwest corner of the chasm trail. To the east the trail slopes
upwards, and to the south four steps have been dug into the solid granite floor
forming a convenient downward path in that direction. These steps are greater in
height than those that one of your stature would normally traverse, which makes
it likely that the architect of this cavern is larger than yourself.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
On and on the chasm trail continues along the northern wall of the chasm. In the
distant east you can see the wall of the cavern as the trail bends to the south
and again to the near west you can see the wall of the cavern where the trail
curves to the south.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
Far below, by the southern wall of the cavern, the floor of the chasm is plainly
visible. From here one can barely distinguish the decaying meat and bones which
are scattered across the chasm floor. A dark shadow seems to swoop across the
carnage as another bone becomes visible.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
From here, the northeast corner of the chasm trail, the bottom of the chasm is
plainly visible as is a large cavern across the chasm. A faint rustling sound
can be heard below the granite floor, yet it cannot be determined where the
sound originates. It is as if the mountain itself was groaning.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
While the pit is some fifty feet deep, the last forty-two feet are filled with a
mixture of water and urine absorbing the impact of anything, or anyone, that has
fallen into the pit. While the details of the sides of the pit are hidden in the
shadows, a few faint indentations in the southern wall can be seen. A smell of
rot and decay fills the air.
name: A Pit

desc:
The cavern comes to a sudden end forming a small underwater room. The water has
come to a standstill, leaving the sandy bottom undisturbed. There is a number of
neatly arranged small rocks in the far corner forming what could be a small nest
of some sort.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
Several tents line the inside of a simple fence to the south and east. The green
grass is thoroughly trampled near the tent entrances and several paths lead
between the tents and towards the north. A long flattish stone lies on the
ground outside a tent to the east, its surface scratched and scarred and
blotched by patches of dark crimson.
name: In a Tent-camp

desc:
A hastily constructed furnace surrounded by a haze of smoke looms in the back of
the tent. A neatly stacked hoard of recently forged weapons is cooling in the
dirt, while next to it lies a pile of damaged pots and pans which have yet to be
mended.
name: Simple Smithy

desc:
A makeshift wooden building has been set up on a patch of trampled grass.
Armour, weapons, and arrows lie in scattered heaps on the floor. There seems to
be little organization to the layout. The building has a roof and four walls,
but where there should be doors, there are just wide openings, leaving the
interior exposed to the elements.
name: Makeshift Armoury

desc:
This abode appears to have seen better days. The tent walls are threadbare and
tears in the fabric have been stitched and patched in numerous places. The
contents look simple but functional, with bedpiles of animal furs and crude
cooking utensils gathered around a small fireplace in the centre of the tent.
name: Simple Tent

desc:
This tent looks to be the home of at least ten people, judging by the bedrolls
of fur that lie along its walls. There is a stone-ringed fireplace in the
middle, beneath a small hole in the tent's ceiling. Next to each bedroll lie
belongings to the occupant. Old worn saddles, sacks and backpacks, and all the
various items a life in a camp brings with it.
name: Large Tent

desc:
The pit is some four-hundred feet deep. Little details can be discerned of the
side or bottom of the pit in the faint light though the circumference can be
estimated at about five feet. If you could feel the walls, they would be silky
smooth and provide no support for climbing.
name: A Pit

desc:
Having just fallen into the chasm near the bottom of the trail, you begin your
rather quick decent towards the bottom of the chasm. As you fall you can see the
long trail as it circles around the chasm. Ouch, this fall is going to bruise
you!
name: Falling into the Chasm

desc:
Having just fallen into the chasm midway down the trail, you begin your rather
quick decent towards the bottom of the chasm. As you fall you can see the long
trail as it circles around the chasm. Ouch, this fall is going to hurt!
name: Falling into the Chasm
